title: For Trump, Soaring Prices Test Voters’ Finances and Patience
summary: Just months before another election that may hinge on the economy, the war in Iran has sent gas and other goods soaring.

title: To Critics, Trump Remarks Reveal a Billionaire Out of Touch
summary: The president has never pretended to be an ordinary American, but a recent “truth bomb” has opened him to criticism that he doesn’t grasp the economic strain of his war with Iran.

title: U.S. Debt Is Now Bigger Than the Economy. That’s Not the Real Problem.
summary: Washington quickly brushed off a startling milestone in a mounting problem.

title: Trump Flexes Hold on G.O.P., Even as It Braces for Midterm Backlash
summary: The defeat of Senator Bill Cassidy showed the president’s dominance in his party at a moment when a broader range of views about Mr. Trump could be a major liability for November.

title: Cassidy Loses Senate Primary in Louisiana, as Trump Vanquishes G.O.P. Foe
summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, a two-term Republican who voted to convict President Trump in his 2021 impeachment trial, could not muster enough votes to continue to a runoff next month.

title: Kentucky’s Curious Cast of Political Characters
summary: The state, where a rebellious Republican faces a tough primary challenge on Tuesday, has an unpredictable political landscape that has produced a long list of surprising figures.

title: Lamar Alexander Wants Republicans to Stand Up to Trump
summary: In a new memoir, the former senator, governor and cabinet member says President Trump committed an impeachable offense on Jan. 6 and calls on Congress to assert its power.

title: Inside the Secret History of the DeGrange Family
summary: One fateful decision 100 years ago created parallel lives. How does a family broken by the bizarre rules of racism heal itself after three generations apart?

title: How the Reputation Firm Terakeet Failed to Fix an Epstein Friend’s Image
summary: Terakeet, a reputation management firm, used online tricks to downplay the friendship of the Goldman Sachs general counsel, Kathryn Ruemmler, with the sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. It wasn’t enough.

title: The Big Questions About Jeffrey Epstein: What The Times Has Learned
summary: Here is what we know about the sexual predator whose secrets spurred an international reckoning over money, power and complicity. Check back for updates.

title: Want to See the Epstein Files in Print? Here Are the 3,437 Volumes.
summary: Documents related to Jeffrey Epstein, taking up more than 3,400 volumes and weighing over eight tons, have been printed and bound for display in a TriBeCa gallery.

title: The Iran War Is Crippling One of the World’s Wealthiest Nations
summary: Iranian attacks have paralyzed Qatar’s vital gas exports and are stalling the tourism and business pivots that were intended to anchor its future growth.

title: In Iraqi Desert, Two Israeli Outposts Were Kept Secret for Months
summary: Israel spent over a year preparing a covert site in Iraq for its operations against Iran, regional officials say. Iraqi officials later confirmed the existence of a second base.

title: W.H.O. Declares Ebola Outbreak a Global Health Emergency
summary: Just a day after the outbreak was announced, cases were confirmed in the capitals of Uganda and the Democratic Republic of Congo. There is no approved vaccine for the strain.

title: The Creator of ‘Shrek!’ Never Got to See the Memes
summary: The writer and illustrator William Steig lived several lives before his book about a surly ogre became a Hollywood hit.

title: We Need More ‘Rabid Partisans’ Like Shane Massey
summary: Defeating a sclerotic rump of a party is no achievement. Instead, one-party rule enables corruption. It fosters stagnation.

title: What A.I. Did to My College Class
summary: Lessons from the first graduating A.I. class.

title: Senate Ruling Threatens Ballroom Funding in G.O.P. Budget Bill
summary: Democrats announced Saturday night that the Senate’s top parliamentary referee had determined that the $1 billion provision did not comply with budget rules.

title: Some Medicare Patients Can Now Get Free CBD
summary: The Trump administration has authorized a test program to see if the cannabis compound can ease some symptoms and reduce health care costs among older patients.

title: Bulgaria Wins Eurovision Song Contest as Israel Comes Second
summary: Five countries boycotted this year’s contest over Israel’s involvement, but politics took a back seat once the show in Vienna got underway.
